crops.name: Maize
crops.description: Staple food crop in Malawi, accounts for over 60% of calorie intake

varieties.name: SC 301 (Kalulu)
varieties.characteristics: Drought tolerant, compact plant, excellent standability, good for low rainfall areas

varieties.name: SC 303 (Kalulu)
varieties.characteristics: Compact plant type, excellent standability, good husk cover

varieties.name: SC 403 (Kanyani)
varieties.characteristics: High yielding under good management, good disease tolerance

varieties.name: SC 419 (Kanyani)
varieties.characteristics: Excellent yield stability, good adaptation to various conditions

varieties.name: SC 423 (Kanyani)
varieties.characteristics: Good emergence, vigorous early growth, stable yielder

varieties.name: SC 529 (Mbidzi)
varieties.characteristics: Good tolerance to major diseases, strong stalks, good husk cover

varieties.name: SC 537 (Mbidzi)
varieties.characteristics: Excellent disease tolerance, good standability, broad adaptation

varieties.name: SC 627 (Mkango)
varieties.characteristics: Very high yielder under optimal conditions, requires good management

varieties.name: SC 653 (Mkango)
varieties.characteristics: Very high yield potential, good husk cover, excellent tip filling

varieties.name: SC 719 (Njobvu)
varieties.characteristics: Excellent husk cover, good tip filling, strong stalks, high shelling percentage

crops.name: Tomato
crops.description: Important vegetable crop for nutrition and income

varieties.name: Roma VF
varieties.characteristics: Determinate, good for processing, disease resistant

varieties.name: Money Maker
varieties.characteristics: Indeterminate, good for fresh market, long harvesting period

diseases.name: Maize Leaf Blight
diseases.description: Fungal disease caused by Exserohilum turcicum, causes significant yield loss if not controlled
diseases.symptoms: Long elliptical brown lesions on leaves starting from tips, tan centers with dark borders, may have yellow halos
diseases.favorable_conditions: High humidity, moderate temperatures (18-27°C), wet weather

treatments.name: Fungicide Application
treatments.application: Apply fungicide at first sign of disease, repeat every 14 days during wet weather. Use Mancozeb or Azoxystrobin-based products.

treatments.name: Resistant Varieties
treatments.application: Plant resistant varieties like SC 529, SC 653 which have good disease tolerance

treatments.name: Crop Rotation
treatments.application: Rotate with non-host crops like legumes (beans, groundnuts) for at least 2 years

treatments.name: Field Sanitation
treatments.application: Remove and destroy crop residues after harvest, practice proper spacing for air circulation

diseases.name: Tomato Early Blight
diseases.description: Fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani, affects leaves, stems and fruits
diseases.symptoms: Dark brown to black spots with concentric rings (target-like appearance), starting on older leaves, yellowing of surrounding tissue
diseases.favorable_conditions: Warm temperatures (24-29°C), high humidity, plant stress

treatments.name: Remove Infected Leaves
treatments.application: Remove and destroy infected plant parts immediately, sanitize tools between plants

treatments.name: Copper-based Fungicide
treatments.application: Apply preventively or at first symptoms. Use copper oxychloride or copper hydroxide products.

treatments.name: Mulching
treatments.application: Apply mulch to prevent soil splash which spreads the fungus to lower leaves

treatments.name: Proper Spacing
treatments.application: Ensure adequate plant spacing (60-75cm between plants) for good air circulation

diseases.name: Maize Streak Virus
diseases.description: Viral disease transmitted by leafhoppers, causes significant yield reduction
diseases.symptoms: Yellow streaks along leaf veins, starting from base, stunted growth, reduced cob size
diseases.favorable_conditions: Presence of leafhopper vectors, dry conditions favor vector population

treatments.name: Resistant Varieties
treatments.application: Plant resistant varieties. Most modern hybrids have good resistance.

treatments.name: Early Planting
treatments.application: Plant early in the season to avoid peak vector populations

treatments.name: Rogue Infected Plants
treatments.application: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately to prevent spread

techniques.name: Integrated Soil Fertility Management
techniques.description: Combines organic and inorganic fertilizers for sustainable soil health
steps: Apply compost or manure before planting (5-10 tons/ha)
Apply top dressing fertilizer when plants are knee-high
Leave crop residues in the field

techniques.name: Conservation Agriculture
techniques.description: Minimum soil disturbance, permanent soil cover, and crop rotation
principles: Maintain permanent soil cover with mulch

documents.title: The efficiency of maize farming in Malawi: A bootstrapped translog frontier
documents.author: Hardwick Tchale, Johannes Sauer
documents.abstract: This study measures the level and determinants of technical efficiency of maize-based smallholder farmers using a bootstrapped translog stochastic frontier. Results show higher technical efficiency with integrated soil fertility management compared to inorganic fertilizer only.
documents.source: Cahiers d'Economie et de Sociologie Rurales

documents.title: Malawi Maize Growers Guide
documents.author: Ministry of Agriculture
documents.abstract: Comprehensive guide for maize production in Malawi including variety selection, planting techniques, pest management, and post-harvest handling.
